sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Let me check the current state - which targets remain and where the crosses are.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
nodes = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']

for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'W':
        print(f"White dot: {n['boundary']}")

blue_targets = [n['boundary'][0] for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] == 1]
yellow_targets = [n['boundary'][0] for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'Y' and n['pixels'] == 1]
print(f"Blue targets remaining: {blue_targets}")
print(f"Yellow targets remaining: {yellow_targets}")

# Check cross positions
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] == 13:
        print(f"Blue cross arm: {n['boundary']}")
    if n['color'] == 'Y' and n['pixels'] == 11:
        print(f"Yellow cross arm: {n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Actually wait, I need to reconsider. When I pressed SPACE from [24, 48], it switched to yellow. But maybe the collection only happens when the target is at the END of an arm, not anywhere on it. Or maybe SPACE only collects targets that are directly adjacent to the cross arms' endpoints.
